mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing institutional investors to liquidate equity positions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which expands aggregate demand and forces equity valuations higher over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, triggering short-covering rallies that push S&P 500 returns positive within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 5-day S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a win rate below 40 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls increase household income and consumer expenditure, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive equity market returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show zero predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 direction, with an out-of-sample accuracy rate indistinguishable from random chance.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 215.0, inflation expectations detach from central bank targets, forcing anticipated rate hikes that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of quarters where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in real GDP and industrial production confirm broad-based economic strength, reducing recession risk premiums and compelling portfolio managers to increase equity allocations over a one-month window.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and INDPRO yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns, with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, elevated policy rates suppress risk appetite and increase discount rates, causing immediate portfolio de-risking that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations, contradicting the discount rate compression mechanism.
